spec.description:
Open Outbox Relay: Performance & Reliability Overview

This dashboard provides a real-time view of the **Open Outbox Relay** health, throughput, and delivery efficiency. It is designed to monitor the lifecycle of events as they move from persistent storage (Transactional Outbox) to message brokers like Kafka, NATS, or Redis.

### 🚀 Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

* **Throughput (EPS):** Measured in Events Per Second. Highlighting sustained delivery speeds across Success and Failure states.
* **Batching Efficiency:** Tracks the effectiveness of `BATCH_SIZE` tuning, showing how many events are grouped per transaction.
* **Delivery Health:** A visual ratio of successful deliveries vs. failed attempts, allowing for quick identification of downstream broker issues.
* **Latency & Backoff:** Monitors the End-to-End time events spend in the outbox and ensures the relay respects backoff constraints during load.

### 🛠 Prerequisites
* **Collector:** OpenTelemetry (Go SDK) exported to Prometheus.
* **Required Metrics:** `openoutbox_events_total`, `openoutbox_relay_state`, `openoutbox_events_e2e_latency_seconds_bucket`, etc.
* **Template Variable:** This dashboard requires a `$DS_PROMETHEUS` data source variable.

spec.title: Sustained Throughput (EPS)
spec.description:
This panel visualizes the real-time processing rate of events, measured in Events Per Second (EPS).

Success (Green): The rate of events successfully delivered to the publisher.

Failed (Red): The rate of events that encountered an error during delivery or expired their retry attempts.

spec.title: P99 End-to-End Latency
spec.description: The 99th percentile of time taken for an event to travel from the database to the message broker. If this is 5s, it means 99% of your events are delivered within 5 seconds.

spec.title: E2E Latency Distribution
spec.description: Visualizes the density of event delivery times over time to identify latency patterns and outliers. Darker or more intense areas represent higher event volumes, making it easy to distinguish between real-time delivery and backlog processing.

spec.title: Delivery SLO Compliance (<${latency_target}s)
spec.description: The percentage of events successfully delivered within the 10-second target window. This serves as the primary health indicator for system freshness, showing how effectively the relay is meeting real-time processing requirements.

spec.title: Drain Efficiency (Time to Empty)
spec.description: How long it will take to clear the current backlog at the current success rate. An increasing value indicates a bottleneck.

spec.title: Publisher Performance (SLO)
spec.description: This panel monitors the outbound delivery speed by tracking the P99 and Median latency of the publisher (e.g., Kafka or NATS) against your $1s$ SLO. By visualizing the distribution of write times, you can instantly distinguish between isolated jitter (P99 spikes) and total downstream saturation (global latency climb)

spec.title: Publisher Latency Distribution
spec.description: Time spent purely inside the Publisher's Publish method. Measures the latency of the outbound leg (Kafka/NATS/etc). High latency here usually indicates target congestion or network saturation.

spec.title: Storage Operation Latency
spec.description: This panel measures the responsiveness of the underlying database for core operations: Claiming (locking pending events) and Deletion (removing processed events). Because the Outbox pattern is database-intensive, monitoring these P99 latencies is critical for identifying lock contention, index inefficiency, or table bloat before they impact global throughput.

spec.title: Data Freshness
spec.description: This panel tracks the age of the oldest pending event in the outbox. While backlog count measures volume, this metric measures SLA health; a rising age indicates that events are being produced faster than they can be delivered, or that specific "poison pill" events are stuck in the queue. It is the definitive indicator of whether the system is operating in real-time or falling into a lag state.

spec.title: Batch Size Distribution
spec.description: Shows the distribution of batch sizes across all workers. A dense line at the 10,000 mark indicates optimal tuning. 'Noise' at lower levels suggests database lock contention or producer lag.

spec.title: Relay Operational State
spec.description: Monitors the lifecycle state of the Relay Engine. 1 = Active (Healthy), 2 = Paused (Publisher Down), 3 = Error (Critical/Storage Down).

spec.title: Batch Filling Efficiency
spec.description: Measures how close the average batch size is to the configured target ($batch_size). High percentage means fewer database round-trips and better throughput. Low percentage indicates the system is thrashing with small batches.

spec.title: Delivery Error Rate
spec.description: Visualizes the percentage of events that resulted in a terminal failure relative to total processing volume. In a healthy system, this should remain near 0%. Persistent values here indicate downstream broker outages, schema mismatches, or exhausted retries.

spec.title: Failed Delivery Volume
spec.description: Displays the current number of failed event deliveries per second. Shows the absolute impact on the system and downstream consumers.

spec.title: Backlog Saturation: New vs. Retrying
spec.description: Monitors the composition of the outbox. 'New' indicates traffic volume; 'Retrying' indicates delivery or downstream failures

spec.title: Open Outbox Dashboard

spec.name: latency_target
spec.label: SLO Threshold (seconds)
spec.description: SLO Latency Threshold: Defines the upper time limit (in seconds) for "successful" event delivery. The dashboard calculates the percentage of events completed within this window using the nearest available histogram bucket.

spec.name: batch_size
spec.label: Configured Batch Size
spec.description: Defines the target efficiency baseline. It represents the BATCH_SIZE configured in the Relay environment variables.

spec.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
spec.label: Prometheus Data source
spec.description: The main data source for Open Outbox dashboard
